title: Method and system for qr code originated vending
abstract: A method for originating a vending transaction via machine-readable code includes: storing, in a memory of a computing device, an account identifier associated with a transaction account; reading, by an optical imaging device of the computing device, a machine-readable code displayed on a vending machine, wherein the machine-readable code is encoded with at least a device identifier; electronically transmitting, by a transmitting device of the computing device, at least the device identifier and an account identifier to a third party system; and receiving, by a receiving device of the computing device, a transaction confirmation indicating approval of a payment transaction involving the transaction account and the vending machine.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A method for originating a vending transaction via machine-readable code, comprising: storing, in a memory of a computing device, an account identifier associated with a transaction account; reading, by an optical imaging device of the computing device, a machine- readable code displayed on a vending machine, wherein the machine-readable code is encoded with at least a device identifier; electronically transmitting, by a transmitting device of the computing device, at least the device identifier and an account identifier to a third party system; and receiving, by a receiving device of the computing device, a transaction confirmation indicating approval of a payment transaction involving the transaction account and the vending machine. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: displaying, on a display device interfaced with the computing device, one or more product data entries, wherein each product data entry is related to a product available in the vending machine and includes at least a product cost; receiving, by an input device interfaced with the computing device, at least one selected product data entry of the one or more product data entries; and electronically transmitting, by the transmitting device of the computing device, the at least one selected product data entry to the third party system. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more product data entries are encoded in the machine-readable code. 4. The method of claim 2, further comprising: receiving, by the receiving device of the computing device, the one or more product data entries from the third party system. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein the transaction confirmation further includes a vending ticket, the vending ticket being comprised of at least the device identifier and one or more product identifiers. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising: electronically transmitting, by the transmitting device of the computing device, the vending ticket to the vending machine. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the transmitting device of the computing device is configured to electronically transmit data to the third party system using a first communication protocol and electronically transmit data to the vending machine using a second communication protocol. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the first communication protocol is one of: the Internet and cellular communication, and 

 the second communication protocol is one of: Bluetooth, radio frequency, and near field communication. 9. A system for originating a vending transaction via machine-readable code, comprising: a memory of a computing device configured to store an account identifier associated with a transaction account; an optical imaging device of the computing device configured to read a machine-readable code displayed on a vending machine, wherein the machine- readable code is encoded with at least a device identifier; a transmitting device of the computing device configured to electronically transmit at least the device identifier and an account identifier to a third party system; and a receiving device of the computing device configured to receive a transaction confirmation indicating approval of a payment transaction involving the transaction account and the vending machine. 10. The system of claim 9, further comprising: a display device interfaced with the computing device configured to display one or more product data entries, wherein each product data entry is related to a product available in the vending machine and includes at least a product cost; and an input device interfaced with the computing device configured to receive at least one selected product data entry of the one or more product data entries, wherein 

 the transmitting device of the computing device is further configured to electronically transmit the at least one selected product data entry to the third party system. 1 1 . The system of claim 10, wherein the one or more product data entries are encoded in the machine-readable code. 12. The system of claim 10, wherein the receiving device of the computing device is further configured to receive the one or more product data entries from the third party system. 13. The system of claim 9, wherein the transaction confirmation further includes a vending ticket, the vending ticket being comprised of at least the device identifier and one or more product identifiers. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the transmitting device of the computing device is further configured to electronically transmit the vending ticket to the vending machine. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein the transmitting device of the computing device is further configured to electronically transmit data to the third party system using a first communication protocol and electronically transmit data to the vending machine using a second communication protocol. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the first communication protocol is one of: the Internet and cellular communication, and the second communication protocol is one of: Bluetooth, radio frequency, and near field communication.
